summary:
• Venezuela's oil output recovery through the PDVSA-Repsol partnership and US-Iran sanctions relief create significant downward pressure on global crude prices, with traders betting improved geopolitical relations will further ease supply constraints.

• US crude inventory drawdowns of 52 million barrels over nine weeks signal tightening domestic supply balances that could support prices, though this is being offset by Hormuz Strait reopening and expectations of increased Iranian production under the US deal.

• Declining Australian LNG production from strikes and Norway's efforts to extend aging North Sea oil field operations highlight structural supply constraints in key non-OPEC regions, limiting upstream growth capacity even as demand-side pressures from China's fuel market weakness persist.
